content:
```diff
--- /dev/null
+++ packages/ui/src/engine/day-night-cycle-design.md
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+# Day/Night Cycle Visual Theme Design Document
+
+## Overview
+This document specifies the design for the day/night cycle visual theme in the pixel engine.
+The cycle lasts 5 minutes total, with distinct phases for day, sunset, night, and sunrise.
+Visual effects include ambient color shifts, overlays, and torch/window content changes.
+
+---
+
+## Cycle Timing
+- Total cycle duration: 5 minutes (300 seconds)
+  - Day: 2.5 minutes (150 seconds)
+  - Sunset transition: 30 seconds
+  - Night: 2 minutes (120 seconds)
+  - Sunrise transition: 30 seconds
+
+The cycle repeats continuously.
+
+---
+
+## Visual Modes and Effects
+
+### Day Mode (2.5 minutes)
+- Ambient lighting: warm white
+  - Approximate ambient color: RGB(255, 244, 214) (warm white)
+- Torches: subtle glow effect, low intensity
+- Window content: normal (no stars)
+- Overlay: none (fully transparent)
+
+### Sunset Transition (30 seconds)
+- Ambient color shifts gradually from warm white to dark blue overlay
+  - Color shift example: interpolate from RGB(255,244,214) to RGB(0,0,40)
+- Overlay opacity increases from 0 to 0.4 (dark blue tint)
+- Torches glow intensifies gradually
+- Window content begins to show stars subtly
+
+### Night Mode (2 minutes)
+- Ambient lighting: dark blue overlay applied as final compositing step
+  - Overlay color: rgba(0, 0, 40, 0.4)
+- Torches: prominent glow, high intensity
+- Window content: stars fully visible
+
+### Sunrise Transition (30 seconds)
+- Ambient color shifts from dark blue overlay back to warm white
+  - Overlay opacity decreases from 0.4 to 0
+- Torches glow diminishes gradually to subtle
+- Window content stars fade out
+
+---
+
+## Overlay Application
+- The dark blue overlay (rgba(0,0,40,0.4)) is applied as a final compositing step after all rooms and elements are drawn.
+- During transitions, overlay opacity interpolates smoothly between 0 and 0.4.
+
+---
+
+## Color Shift Values Summary
+| Phase          | Ambient Color (RGB) | Overlay Color (RGBA)    | Overlay Opacity | Torch Glow Intensity | Window Stars Visibility |
+|----------------|---------------------|------------------------|-----------------|----------------------|------------------------|
+| Day            | (255, 244, 214)     | (0, 0, 0, 0)           | 0               | Low                  | None                   |
+| Sunset         | Interpolating       | (0, 0, 40, 0 to 0.4)   | 0 → 0.4         | Increasing           | Increasing              |
+| Night          | (0, 0, 40) overlay  | (0, 0, 40, 0.4)        | 0.4             | High                 | Full                   |
+| Sunrise        | Interpolating       | (0, 0, 40, 0.4 to 0)   | 0.4 → 0         | Decreasing           | Decreasing              |
+
+---
+
+## Notes
+- Torch glow and window star visibility should be controlled by the rendering engine based on the current phase.
+- Smooth interpolation during transitions ensures a natural visual effect.
+- Overlay compositing must be the last rendering step to ensure consistent tinting.
```
